# Infrastructure ‚Äî Pulumi + Azure

The Pulumi program at `infra/app/` provisions all Azure resources. Full setup guide: `infra/README.md`.

## MCP Tool-Use

**Always prefer tools over guessing.**

### Decision Router

| Question | Tool | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Limits, SKUs, availability, docs, code samples | **Microsoft Learn MCP** | VS Code extension (built-in) |
| List/query Azure resources (subscriptions, RGs, Cosmos accounts‚Ä¶) | **Azure MCP** | `.vscode/mcp.json` |
| Current deployed state, outputs, diffs | Terminal: `pulumi preview` / `pulumi stack output` | CLI |

### Microsoft Learn MCP ‚Äî Azure docs, service limits, SKUs, and code samples

> Built into the [GitHub Copilot for Azure](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-azuretools.vscode-azure-github-copilot) VS Code extension.

- Need API reference, service limits, SKU availability, or pricing ‚Üí `microsoft_docs_search` then `microsoft_docs_fetch` for the full page.
- Need to check if an Azure resource type, SKU, or feature is available in a region ‚Üí search Azure docs first; don't assume availability.
- Generating Azure SDK code ‚Üí `microsoft_code_sample_search` with `language: python`.

### Azure MCP ‚Äî live Azure resource management

> Configured in `.vscode/mcp.json` via `@azure/mcp`. Works for everyone with `npx` ‚Äî no extension required.

- Use for querying subscriptions, resource groups, Cosmos DB accounts, Container Apps, Key Vault, Service Bus, and 40+ other Azure services.
- Authenticates via your `az login` session. Ensure you are logged into the correct tenant.
- Management-plane queries (list accounts, RGs) work out of the box. Data-plane queries (e.g., Cosmos DB items) require your IP to be allowed by any resource firewalls.

### Rules
- If a tool can answer precisely, **use it** instead of guessing.
- When the answer depends on current state, **never hallucinate** ‚Äî verify via MCP or terminal.
- For Azure service details: Microsoft Learn MCP first. For live resource state: Azure MCP first.

## Project Structure

```
infra/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ app/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ __main__.py              # Pulumi program ‚Äî resource orchestration
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Pulumi.yml               # Project config: runtime=python, toolchain=uv
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Pulumi.dev-<name>.yml    # Per-developer stack config
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Pulumi.staging.yml
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Pulumi.production.yml
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ pyproject.toml           # Dependencies (uv path sources ‚Üí common libs)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ common/libs/                 # Synced from fde-core
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ core/                    # ms.infra.core ‚Äî Settings, naming, base models
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ utils/                   # ms.infra.utils ‚Äî deploy_*, config models, roles
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ docker/
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ fastapi/container-app/Dockerfile
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ vite/container-app/Dockerfile
```

### Ownership & Common Libs Workflow

- `infra/app/` ‚Äî **owned by this repo**. Safe to edit.
- `infra/common/libs/` ‚Äî **synced from `microsoft/fde-core`**. Will be overwritten on next bot sync.

**To modify common libs** (`ms.infra.core` or `ms.infra.utils`):
1. Make changes locally in `infra/common/libs/core/` or `infra/common/libs/utils/`.
2. Run tests: `cd infra/common/libs/core && uv run pytest` (same for `utils`).
3. Once tests pass, open a PR to `microsoft/fde-core` with the same changes.
4. Your local changes work as a staging copy until the `fde-core` PR is merged and synced back.

## Stacks & Environments

| Stack | Purpose | Backend | Deployment |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dev-<name>` | Per-developer sandbox | Local or Pulumi Cloud | Manual: `pulumi up --stack dev-<name>` |
| `staging` | Integration testing | Pulumi Cloud (OIDC) | Auto on merge to `main` |
| `production` | Live | Pulumi Cloud (OIDC) | Manual trigger + plan approval |

Dev stack quick setup:
```bash
cd infra/app
az account set --subscription <your-subscription-id>
pulumi login --local
export PULUMI_CONFIG_PASSPHRASE=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
echo "export PULUMI_CONFIG_PASSPHRASE=$PULUMI_CONFIG_PASSPHRASE" >> ~/.zshrc  # or ~/.bashrc
pulumi stack select dev-<name> --create
pulumi up
```

CI workflow: PR ‚Üí `pulumi preview staging` (PR comment) ‚Üí merge ‚Üí auto `pulumi up staging` ‚Üí manual production (with plan approval).

## Best Practices

### General Pulumi
- One resource group per stack.
- Always `pulumi preview` before `pulumi up`. Review the diff.
- Export meaningful outputs (endpoints, connection strings, principal IDs).
- Use `pulumi.Output.apply()` for transformations. Never call `.get()` on Pulumi `Output` values (no blocking).
- Never hardcode resource IDs, endpoints, or names. Wire through outputs and config.
- Keep secrets in Pulumi config: `pulumi config set --secret <key> <value>`.
- Fail fast at preview time. Validate config during preview, not deploy (see `_cosmos_container()` pattern).

### Autonaming & Naming
- **Prefer Pulumi autonaming everywhere.** Let Pulumi generate resource names with random suffixes ‚Äî this makes stack renames and resource migrations painless.
- All stacks set `pulumi:autonaming` in config YAML ‚Äî always include a unique prefix for your dev stack.
- **Avoid baking project or stack names into resource names.** Hardcoded name patterns (e.g., `rg-{project}-{stack}`) create coupling that blocks renames.
- Explicit names only where Azure strictly requires globally unique deterministic names (ACR, storage accounts) via `ms.infra.core.naming` helper functions.
- Use `generate_acr_name()` and `generate_storage_account_name()` for those cases ‚Äî never construct names manually.

### Configuration
- All typed config loaded via `COMMON_CONFIG = CommonPulumiConfig.from_pulumi_config()` (module-level singleton).
- Config defined in `Pulumi.<stack>.yml`, deserialized into frozen Pydantic models.
- Never read raw `pulumi.Config()` directly in `__main__.py`. Always use `COMMON_CONFIG`.
- New config key: add field to model in `ms.infra.utils.models` ‚Üí update `from_pulumi_config()` ‚Üí add to stack YAMLs.

### Security & Identity
- Always use Managed Identity. Never use keys or connection strings for Cosmos, OpenAI, or Service Bus.
- Use `BuiltInRole` enum for RBAC. Never hardcode role definition GUIDs.
- Cosmos uses SQL Role Assignments for **data-plane** access (not standard RBAC) via `assign_cosmos_data_contributor()`. Management-plane still uses standard RBAC.
- Scope role assignments to the minimum level (resource, not resource group).
- Never log or export secrets. ACR admin credentials are wrapped in `Output.secret()`.

### Container Apps
- Shared `ManagedEnvironment` with Log Analytics integration.
- Build + push images via `build_docker_image()`. Never build outside Pulumi.
- Health probes auto-generated from `ContainerApp.health_check_path`.
- Internal routing: frontend ‚Üí backend via internal FQDN (`fqdn.replace(".", ".internal.", 1)`), not external HTTPS.
- Env vars assembled as `dict[str, str | pulumi.Output[Any]]`.

### Resource Provisioning Order
1. Resource Group ‚Üí 2. Observability ‚Üí 3. AI Foundry ‚Üí 4. Cosmos DB ‚Üí 5. Service Bus ‚Üí 6. Container Apps (ACR ‚Üí ManagedEnvironment ‚Üí backend ‚Üí frontend)

OpenAI model deployments are chained sequentially (`depends_on=[prev]`) to avoid concurrent provisioning failures.

## Shared Utility Libraries

### `ms.infra.utils` ‚Äî Deploy Functions

Always use these instead of raw `pulumi_azure_native` resources.

| Function | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `deploy_cosmos_nosql(resource_group, *, database_name, containers)` | Serverless Cosmos DB + database + containers (optional vector search) |
| `assign_cosmos_data_contributor(...)` | Cosmos SQL role assignment |
| `deploy_observability(resource_group, *, retention_in_days)` | Log Analytics + App Insights |
| `deploy_service_bus(resource_group, *, queues, sku)` | Service Bus namespace + queues |
| `deploy_foundry_with_project(resource_group)` | AI Foundry account + project + OpenAI deployments |
| `create_and_login_acr(resource_group, admin_user_enabled)` | ACR + `az acr login` |
| `build_docker_image(acr, acr_login, app)` | Build + push Docker image to ACR |
| `create_single_container_app(...)` | Single Container App with probes, scaling, MI, ACR auth |
| `create_container_apps(resource_group, env_vars, ...)` | All apps from config with shared env |

### `ms.infra.utils.models` ‚Äî Config Models (frozen Pydantic)

| Model | Key Fields |
|---|---|
| `CommonPulumiConfig` | Top-level singleton via `COMMON_CONFIG` |
| `ContainerApp` | `app_name`, `dockerfile_path`, `docker_build_context`, `replicas_min/max`, `cpu`, `memory_in_gb`, `health_check_path` |
| `CosmosConfig` / `CosmosContainerConfig` | `database_name`, `containers`, optional `vector` config |
| `AzureOpenAiModel` | `model_name`, `deployment_name`, `capacity`, `version`, `sku_name`, `role?` |
| `ServiceBusConfig` / `ServiceBusQueueConfig` | `queues` with `max_delivery_count`, `dead_lettering` |
| `RoleAssignmentInfo` | `name`, `role: BuiltInRole`, `scope` ‚Äî has `.create_service_principal_role_assignment()` |

### `ms.infra.core` ‚Äî Settings & Naming

| API | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `settings` (singleton) | `subscription_id`, `tenant_id`, `stack_name`, `is_dev_stack`, `is_staging_stack`, `is_production_stack`, `repo_root`, `build_id` |
| `generate_acr_name(...)` | Deterministic ACR name (one of the few cases needing explicit names) |
| `generate_storage_account_name(...)` | Deterministic 24-char storage account name |
| `FrozenBaseModel` | Base for all config models (`frozen=True`) |

### `BuiltInRole` / `CosmosSqlRole` Enums

`COGNITIVE_SERVICES_OPENAI_USER`, `SERVICE_BUS_DATA_SENDER`, `ACR_PUSH`, `ACR_PULL`, `STORAGE_BLOB_DATA_CONTRIBUTOR`, `CosmosSqlRole.DATA_CONTRIBUTOR`

## Architecture ‚Äî What Gets Provisioned

| Resource | Detail |
|---|---|
| Resource Group | One per stack (autonamed) |
| Cosmos DB | NoSQL serverless, database + containers (optional vector search) |
| AI Foundry | OpenAI account + project, model deployments (configured in stack YAML) |
| Service Bus | Standard, queues (configured in stack YAML) |
| App Insights + Log Analytics | 30-day retention |
| Container Apps | Backend (FastAPI) + frontend (Vite/nginx), shared ManagedEnvironment, ACR, system MI |

## Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Fix |
|---|---|
| Editing `infra/common/libs/` directly | Test locally, then PR to `microsoft/fde-core` |
| Using raw `pulumi_azure_native` resources | Use `deploy_*` from `ms.infra.utils` |
| Reading `pulumi.Config()` in `__main__.py` | Use `COMMON_CONFIG` |
| Hardcoding names, endpoints, or IDs | Wire through Pulumi outputs and config |
| Key-based auth for Cosmos/OpenAI/SB | Managed Identity + RBAC |
| Hardcoding role definition GUIDs | Use `BuiltInRole` / `CosmosSqlRole` enums |
| Deploying without preview | Always `pulumi preview` first |
| Dev stack without autonaming | Set `pulumi:autonaming` pattern |
| Baking project/stack names into resource names | Rely on autonaming + random suffix; explicit names only for ACR/storage |
| Manual ACR/storage names | Use `generate_acr_name()` / `generate_storage_account_name()` |

## Adding a New Azure Resource

1. Check if a `deploy_*` function exists in `ms.infra.utils`. Use it if so.
2. Not there? Check `fde-core` common libs. If found, PR to `fde-core`.
3. If truly new:
   - Add frozen Pydantic config model to `ms.infra.utils.models`.
   - Add `deploy_*` function in `ms.infra.utils`.
   - Add config to all `Pulumi.<stack>.yml` files.
   - Update `CommonPulumiConfig.from_pulumi_config()`.
   - Wire outputs to env vars in `__main__.py` if needed.
   - Export outputs at end of `__main__.py`.
   - `pulumi preview` to validate.

## Key Commands

```bash
cd infra/app
pulumi preview --stack dev-<name>    # always preview first
pulumi up --stack dev-<name>         # deploy
pulumi stack output --stack dev-<name>
pulumi config --stack dev-<name>
pulumi destroy --stack dev-<name>    # cleanup
uv sync                              # install deps
uv run pyright                       # type check
uv run pytest                        # tests
```
